seoTitle: How to Connect Trello to Jira
metaDescription: Link Jira issues to Trello cards using the native Jira Power-Up, sync statuses, and attach epics, bugs, and sprints without switching apps.
intro: Syncing high-level planning boards in Trello with your engineering team's Jira tracker doesn't have to mean double data entry. This guide covers connecting the two platforms with the native Jira Power-Up, so you can track live development status right from Trello.

steps.heading: Open the Power-Up directory
steps.body: On your target board, click Power-Ups or Apps in the header to open the directory.

steps.heading: Add the Jira Power-Up
steps.body: Search for Jira, locate the official Atlassian Power-Up, and click Add.

steps.heading: Authorize your account
steps.body: Click the configuration gear icon on the module and select Authorize Account, then complete the security handshake.

steps.heading: Select your Jira instance
steps.body: Confirm your admin credentials and choose the specific Jira Cloud instance you want to sync.

steps.heading: Attach an issue to a card
steps.body: Open a target card and find the new Jira action button to search for an existing issue or create a new one.

steps.heading: Monitor live status badges
steps.body: Check the front of your card for a color-coded Jira status badge, and click the linked ticket to jump directly into Jira.

faq.question: Do I need separate logins for Trello and Jira?
faq.answer: You'll authorize a connection once between your Atlassian account and Trello workspace, after which the integration handles the sync.

faq.question: What Jira items can I attach to a Trello card?
faq.answer: You can attach epics, stories, bugs, and active sprints directly to a Trello card.

faq.question: Will I see Jira updates without leaving Trello?
faq.answer: Yes, cards display a live color-coded status badge that reflects the current state of the linked Jira issue.

faq.question: Can I create a brand-new Jira issue from Trello?
faq.answer: Yes, the Jira Power-Up lets you create a new ticket directly from a Trello card without switching apps.
